title: Operator (FM Synthesis)
summary: Ableton's FM synthesizer. Uses oscillators that modulate each other to create complex timbres. Used in Kettama's production for distinctive bass and organ sounds. Suite only.
steps: Operator has 4 oscillators (A, B, C, D). The Algorithm selector determines how they connect
In most algorithms: the bottom oscillator (D) modulates upward through C → B → A. Oscillator A is the carrier — the audible output. B, C, D are modulators — they affect timbre only
Algorithm 1 (all in series D→C→B→A): the simplest — one carrier, three modulators stacked. Good for bass starting point
Frequency Ratio: the pitch relationship between oscillators. Ratio 1.00 = oscillator plays the same pitch as the MIDI note. Ratio 2.00 = one octave up. Ratio 0.5 = one octave down
Modulation Amount: how much each modulator affects its carrier. Low amount = few extra harmonics (sine-like). High amount = complex, metallic timbre
For an FM bass: Oscillator A ratio 1.00, envelope fast attack/decay (plucky). Oscillator B ratio 1.00, Amount 20–40% (adds 2nd harmonic body)
tips: FM synthesis creates harmonics by math, not by filtering — the interaction of frequency ratios determines the timbre. Integer ratios (1, 2, 3) = harmonic sounds. Non-integer (1.5, 2.37) = inharmonic/metallic
Start with Algorithm 1 and only oscillators A+B active. Understand the two-operator relationship before adding C and D
FM bass with fast B envelope: the modulation is only active at the start of the note (the attack), creating a click/pluck. The tail is clean sine. This is the classic 80s FM bass character
Operator is Suite only — if you have Live Standard, use Serum V2 for FM-like sounds via the FM from B feature in Serum's oscillator section
practice_hint: Operator is Suite-only. If you have it: open Operator, select Algorithm 1, enable only A and B oscillators. Set B Amount to 30%. Hold a low note and slowly increase B's Amount from 0 to 80% — hear the timbre change.
ableton_version_note: Operator is available in Ableton Live Suite only. Not available in Standard.
